summary: hello244a exfiltrates SSH keys, PEM certs, and.env files from CI hosts via postinstall
details: The package.json postinstall lifecycle script writes a shell payload to /tmp/x.sh that uses `docker run --rm -v /:/host alpine` to bind-mount the entire host filesystem into a container, then recursively scans /host/home/runner/work, /host/tmp, and /host/var/tmp for.env, id_rsa, *.pem, and config.json files. The collected credential material is base64-encoded and POSTed via wget to https://eodxy50gl486xrx.m.pipedream.net/?cross_tenant=1. The docker-bind-mount escape indicates the payload specifically targets CI runners (e.g. GitHub Actions runners with docker socket access) to reach beyond the install sandbox and steal SSH keys, TLS/PEM certificates, environment secrets, and JSON config files from the host. Behavior fires automatically on `npm install`.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
